name: lens_gain
purpose: Expose spatial lens-shading compensation and its noise amplification context.
expected_effect: Lets downstream gating distinguish center and vignetted regions when a calibrated gain map exists.
algorithm: Nearest-resize the calibration lens_shading_gain map and multiply it into gain-corrected RAW.
value_semantics: Positive gain; 1 means neutral and values above 1 amplify signal and modeled noise.
applicability: Meaningful only with a sensor/lens calibration map.
limitations: A uniform value of 1 is missing/neutral calibration, not proof of uniform optics.

name: defect_confidence
purpose: Mark pixels replaced from the defect table.
expected_effect: Allows DNN and health logic to reduce trust in interpolated sensor samples.
algorithm: Initialize ones and set calibrated defect coordinates to zero after local box-filter replacement.
value_semantics: [0,1]; 0 marks a corrected defect and 1 an unlisted pixel.
applicability: Requires an accurate defect-pixel table.
limitations: An all-one map means no table entries were supplied, not that the sensor has no defects.

name: hdr_exposure_source
purpose: Identify the exposure with the largest fusion weight at each pixel.
expected_effect: Makes exposure switching auditable and can condition highlight/shadow processing.
algorithm: Argmax of SNR × non-saturation × low-signal × inter-plane-consistency weights; use the darkest fallback when every weight is zero.
value_semantics: Categorical exposure-plane index 0..E-1.
applicability: Useful for multi-exposure inputs; single exposure is uniformly index 0.
limitations: It is the dominant contributor, not a hard statement that other planes were unused.

name: saturation
purpose: Locate pixels saturated in any input exposure.
expected_effect: Supports highlight-risk gating and prevents saturated structure from receiving high edge confidence.
algorithm: Threshold every normalized exposure and reduce with logical any across the exposure axis.
value_semantics: Binary [0,1]; 1 means at least one source plane crossed hdr_saturation_threshold.
applicability: Applies to single and multi-exposure RAW.
limitations: It does not mean the fused HDR result is clipped; a shorter plane may still recover the pixel.

name: clipping_distance
purpose: Express remaining headroom below the saturation threshold.
expected_effect: Provides a continuous highlight-risk signal beyond the binary saturation map.
algorithm: Normalize threshold minus the maximum normalized exposure by the threshold and clip to [0,1].
value_semantics: [0,1]; 0 is at/above threshold and larger values have more headroom.
applicability: Applies to the configured pseudo-RAW scale.
limitations: It is based on the brightest input plane, not fused-radiance headroom.

name: hdr_confidence
purpose: Summarize usable HDR fusion support.
expected_effect: Allows output and DNN validity to fall where exposures are saturated, noisy, or inconsistent.
algorithm: Sum nonnegative fusion weights and divide by exposure count, then clip to [0,1].
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger aggregate usable exposure support.
applicability: Applies to single and multi-exposure inputs.
limitations: This is a heuristic confidence and is not calibrated probability or HDR quality ground truth.

name: ghost_motion_artifact
purpose: Expose inter-plane radiance disagreement that can create ghosting.
expected_effect: Can suppress unreliable HDR edges and reveal motion/flicker merge risk.
algorithm: Compute per-pixel radiance standard deviation divided by mean absolute radiance, clipped to [0,1].
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means greater normalized disagreement.
applicability: Informative only for two or more exposure planes.
limitations: No registration or optical flow is used; motion, JPEG differences, AE errors, and noise are conflated.

name: luma
purpose: Provide a dense structural intensity channel close to the sensor domain.
expected_effect: Supports low-latency edge and temporal processing without waiting for display rendering.
algorithm: For Bayer use the measured/interpolated green plane; clear, IR, and mono layouts use their decoder-specific intensity.
value_semantics: Nonnegative linear intensity on the fused pseudo-RAW scale.
applicability: Applicable to all supported CFA modes.
limitations: Bayer green is a luma proxy, not colorimetric luminance.

name: clear_channel
purpose: Expose clear-pixel signal for clear-filter CFA layouts.
expected_effect: May improve low-light structure when genuine clear pixels and calibration are available.
algorithm: Interpolate C samples for RCCB/RCCC/RCCG; use fused signal for mono; emit zeros for Bayer/RGB-IR.
value_semantics: Nonnegative linear clear signal; zero when the active CFA has no clear samples.
applicability: Only physically meaningful for native clear/mono CFA capture.
limitations: CameraE2E Bayer and RGB proxy bridges do not create native clear measurements.

name: ir_channel
purpose: Expose interpolated infrared-pixel signal.
expected_effect: Can let a model reason about IR illumination and visible-color contamination.
algorithm: Interpolate IR samples in RGB-IR mode; emit zeros for Bayer, clear, and mono modes.
value_semantics: Nonnegative linear IR signal; zero when the CFA has no IR samples.
applicability: Only physically meaningful for native RGB-IR capture and crosstalk calibration.
limitations: An RGB-derived CameraE2E proxy is not a native IR measurement.

name: demosaic_confidence
purpose: Estimate reliability of reconstructed color samples.
expected_effect: Supports artifact suppression and downweights uncertain color edges.
algorithm: Combine CFA neighborhood support, horizontal/vertical gradient separation and local contrast, then mix with edge confidence and non-edge support.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher indicates stronger reconstruction support.
applicability: Decoder-specific; valid as a relative diagnostic for supported CFA modes.
limitations: Not calibrated against demosaic ground truth and partly depends on later edge confidence.

name: color_confidence
purpose: Estimate where color information is trustworthy.
expected_effect: Can reduce reliance on chroma under low SNR, saturation, poor HDR support, or strong global imbalance.
algorithm: Multiply normalized SNR, non-saturation, global WB-balance confidence, and HDR confidence.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger modeled color reliability.
applicability: Applicable to decoded RGB but depends on calibration quality.
limitations: Global channel imbalance is only a WB heuristic and can penalize legitimately colored scenes.

name: log_r_over_g
purpose: Represent red chromaticity relative to green.
expected_effect: Offers illumination/color cues with reduced dependence on overall brightness.
algorithm: Compute safe log(R/G) after the camera color matrix.
value_semantics: Signed log ratio; 0 means R equals G.
applicability: Applicable when decoded camera RGB is meaningful.
limitations: Sensitive to color matrix, demosaic error, clipping, and very small green values.

name: log_b_over_g
purpose: Represent blue chromaticity relative to green.
expected_effect: Offers illumination/color cues with reduced dependence on overall brightness.
algorithm: Compute safe log(B/G) after the camera color matrix.
value_semantics: Signed log ratio; 0 means B equals G.
applicability: Applicable when decoded camera RGB is meaningful.
limitations: Sensitive to color matrix, demosaic error, clipping, and very small green values.

name: ir_contamination
purpose: Estimate the fraction of signal attributable to IR.
expected_effect: Can gate visible-color features where IR leakage is strong.
algorithm: Compute IR divided by mean visible RGB plus IR.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means larger modeled IR contribution.
applicability: Only meaningful for genuine RGB-IR input.
limitations: Uniform zero on Bayer is not evidence of an IR-free physical sensor.

name: noise_variance
purpose: Expose modeled per-pixel uncertainty in the fused RAW signal.
expected_effect: Allows denoising, fast-path features, and DNN inputs to distinguish signal from noisy regions.
algorithm: Sum shot, read, temperature/exposure dark-current, quantization, and calibration-residual variance; scale by lens_gain squared.
value_semantics: Positive modeled variance in normalized signal units squared.
applicability: Requires representative sensor-noise and calibration coefficients.
limitations: CameraE2E bridge defaults are not a calibrated nuScenes camera noise model.

name: snr_map
purpose: Provide bounded signal reliability.
expected_effect: Selected as the compact reliability channel in the stable RGB+Aux tensor and as a gate for edge/color confidence.
algorithm: Compute signal/sqrt(variance), then map SNR to SNR/(SNR+1).
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger modeled signal relative to noise.
applicability: Relative validity follows the supplied noise model.
limitations: It is not measured SNR and is optimistic or pessimistic when calibration coefficients are wrong.

name: noise_normalized_gradient
purpose: Measure local intensity change relative to expected noise.
expected_effect: Helps distinguish structured transitions from noise-only gradients.
algorithm: Divide luma gradient magnitude by sqrt(2 × modeled variance).
value_semantics: Nonnegative, unbounded standardized gradient-like score.
applicability: Useful when both luma and the noise model are meaningful.
limitations: Large texture and demosaic artifacts can also produce high values.

name: edge_strength
purpose: Represent local structural gradient magnitude.
expected_effect: Supports boundary-aware image formation, sparse EdgePackets, and DNN spatial features.
algorithm: Build a noise-weighted luma/R-G/B-G structure tensor, take sqrt of the largest eigenvalue, and normalize by the frame maximum.
value_semantics: [0,1] frame-relative strength.
applicability: Applicable to all decoded inputs.
limitations: Frame-max normalization prevents absolute comparison across unrelated runs without stored statistics.

name: edge_orientation
purpose: Expose the dominant local edge direction.
expected_effect: Can support orientation-aware filtering or geometric feature extraction.
algorithm: Use half-angle atan2 of the structure-tensor off-diagonal and diagonal difference.
value_semantics: Cyclic radians in approximately [-pi/2, pi/2].
applicability: Meaningful where edge strength/confidence is sufficient.
limitations: Orientation is unstable in flat, noisy, or isotropic texture regions.

name: edge_confidence
purpose: Estimate whether a detected edge is structurally and sensor-wise reliable.
expected_effect: Expected to suppress noisy, saturated, blurred, or isotropic edges while retaining supported boundaries.
algorithm: Multiply structure-tensor anisotropy, MTF, exp(-0.5×PSF sigma squared), non-saturation, and SNR.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger modeled edge reliability.
applicability: Depends on valid noise and optics calibration.
limitations: A high value is not object-boundary probability or detector performance evidence.

name: edge_evidence
purpose: Combine edge magnitude and reliability into one compact feature.
expected_effect: Selected for DNN/proposal experiments where strength alone admits noise and confidence alone can be weak at strong boundaries.
algorithm: Robust-normalize strength and confidence with p1/p99 ranges and take their geometric mean.
value_semantics: [0,1] frame-relative combined evidence.
applicability: Applicable to decoded inputs with meaningful edge maps.
limitations: Percentile normalization makes values run-relative rather than calibrated probabilities.

name: edge_type
purpose: Separate luminance and chromatic edge evidence.
expected_effect: Can distinguish intensity boundaries from color-only transitions and demosaic-sensitive locations.
algorithm: Normalize luma and maximum chroma gradient by frame maxima and classify at threshold 0.12 as none/luma/chroma/both.
value_semantics: Categorical: 0 none, 1 luma, 2 chroma, 3 both.
applicability: Applicable when decoded chroma differences are meaningful.
limitations: Fixed frame-relative thresholds are diagnostic and not universally calibrated.

name: blur_focus_confidence
purpose: Summarize local focus support from strong reliable edges.
expected_effect: Can flag regions where detail-sensitive downstream features should be downweighted.
algorithm: Average edge confidence with edge strength normalized by the configured high-percentile strength threshold.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger local focus-like support.
applicability: Relative within a frame and most meaningful around edges.
limitations: Texture scarcity, motion blur, defocus, and low contrast are not separated.

name: mtf_confidence
purpose: Carry calibrated spatial MTF reliability into edge processing.
expected_effect: Allows known lens/sensor resolution loss to reduce edge confidence.
algorithm: Nearest-resize calibration.mtf_confidence_map, defaulting to one.
value_semantics: Normally [0,1]; one means no attenuation in the model.
applicability: Meaningful only with measured/calibrated MTF data.
limitations: Uniform one is a neutral default, not measured perfect MTF.

name: psf_sigma
purpose: Expose calibrated local PSF blur scale.
expected_effect: Lets downstream logic reason about spatially varying optical blur.
algorithm: Nearest-resize calibration.psf_sigma_map and clamp to nonnegative values.
value_semantics: Nonnegative sigma in calibration-defined sensor-pixel units.
applicability: Meaningful only with calibrated PSF data.
limitations: Zero is the default when no PSF map is supplied and is not a measured diffraction-free result.

name: psf_blur_confidence
purpose: Convert PSF width into a bounded sharpness prior.
expected_effect: Expected to reduce trust in edges where calibrated blur is large.
algorithm: Compute exp(-0.5 × psf_sigma squared).
value_semantics: [0,1]; 1 at sigma 0 and decreasing with blur scale.
applicability: Depends entirely on PSF calibration.
limitations: A uniform one usually reflects missing/default PSF calibration.

name: psf_edge_likelihood
purpose: Prefer reliable edges that are not explained by HDR disagreement.
expected_effect: Selected as a compact edge/optics cue for DNN experiments under blur and multi-exposure stress.
algorithm: Subtract ghost_motion_artifact from edge_confidence and clip to [0,1].
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means confident edge with low inter-plane disagreement.
applicability: Useful as a diagnostic fusion of edge and HDR consistency.
limitations: It is not a fitted PSF likelihood despite the historical name.

name: rolling_row_fraction
purpose: Expose normalized sensor-row readout position.
expected_effect: Allows models or diagnostics to condition on rolling-readout timing.
algorithm: Generate a linear 0..1 vector over rows and reverse it for bottom-to-top readout.
value_semantics: One-dimensional [0,1] row coordinate.
applicability: Requires correct readout direction; row timestamps are available in Trace.
limitations: No rolling-shutter geometric compensation is performed.

name: temporal_difference
purpose: Expose change from the previous processed frame.
expected_effect: Supports low-latency motion/change awareness and frozen-frame diagnostics.
algorithm: Absolute difference between current linear perception luma and PreviousFrameState luma; zero without compatible state.
value_semantics: Nonnegative linear intensity difference.
applicability: Requires correctly chained same-camera PreviousFrameState.
limitations: No motion compensation; ego/object motion, AE, JPEG artifacts, and flicker are mixed.

name: temporal_consistency
purpose: Convert frame change into bounded stability.
expected_effect: Allows downstream paths to downweight temporally unstable regions.
algorithm: Compute exp(-temporal_difference / temporal_flicker_threshold).
value_semantics: [0,1]; 1 is unchanged and lower values indicate larger change.
applicability: Requires compatible previous-frame state.
limitations: It measures photometric consistency, not correspondence or optical flow confidence.

name: led_flicker_confidence
purpose: Highlight bright temporally unstable or HDR-disagreeing regions.
expected_effect: Intended to expose LED/flicker risk to runtime control and downstream gating.
algorithm: Combine (1-temporal_consistency) × bright-light gate with HDR disagreement × saturation, then clip.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger flicker-like evidence.
applicability: Most informative for bright light sources in a chained sequence or multi-exposure input.
limitations: Not an LED classifier; motion, exposure change, and compression can raise it.

name: light_source_confidence
purpose: Represent bright light-source-like regions with a flicker penalty.
expected_effect: Can help distinguish stable lights from unstable highlights.
algorithm: Map luma above 0.65 into a 0..1 bright gate and multiply by 1-0.5×flicker.
value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means bright and relatively stable.
applicability: Diagnostic for the configured linear intensity scale.
limitations: Brightness alone cannot distinguish lamps, reflections, sky, or saturated surfaces.
